title: Build an ErrorBoundary Component
prompt: Build an `ErrorBoundary` class component that catches JavaScript errors in its children and displays a fallback UI instead. It should expose a `fallback` prop (ReactNode) and a `onError` optional callback. After catching an error, provide a 'Try Again' button that resets the boundary.
context: Error Boundaries must be class components — they use `getDerivedStateFromError` (to update state after an error) and `componentDidCatch` (to log the error). Function components cannot implement these lifecycle methods. This is one of the few remaining required uses of class components in React.
